scope: v0.11.1 Hot-path typed recall — MEM-ERA-0111: p_content_types TEXT[] DEFAULT NULL mirrored from recall_hybrid() into recall_fast() and recall_lessons(). recall_2phase does not exist as a SQL function; recall_fast and recall_lessons are the canonical hot-path targets.
justification: Backwards-compatible PATCH. recall_fast() gains a 6th optional parameter p_content_types (default NULL); recall_lessons() gains an 8th optional parameter p_content_types (default NULL). When NULL, both functions behave IDENTICALLY to the prior signatures — no change in default retrieval path, no existing caller affected. When a set of content types is supplied, a pushdown predicate content_type = ANY(p_content_types) is applied in the WHERE clause using ix_pgmnemo_content_type_active, restricting candidates before HNSW ranking. An empty array returns zero rows (explicit exclusion). This is honest parity: typed recall is now usable on the consumer hot path (recall_fast is called by Agency MCP _search_lessons); no recall-quality lift is claimed and none is measured. significance_required=false because this is a filter-path extension, not a ranking change.
schema_change_detail: No column or index changes. recall_fast() and recall_lessons() function signatures extended with trailing defaulted parameters. CREATE OR REPLACE; old call sites resolve unchanged via DEFAULT NULL.
scoring_impact: none on the default path (p_content_types = NULL bypasses the filter entirely). When set, only the candidate set is restricted before HNSW ranking; ranking weights are unchanged.
recall_ranking_impact: none for existing callers. With p_content_types set, results are a content-type-scoped subset of the unfiltered result set; no re-weighting of any signal.

installcheck.typed_recall_fast_test: PASS — output identical to expected (T1-T10 covering both recall_fast and recall_lessons hot-path typed recall)

upgrade_paths.fresh_install_0.11.1_flat: PASS — CREATE EXTENSION pgmnemo VERSION '0.11.1' → version() = '0.11.1'
upgrade_paths.alter_0.11.0_to_0.11.1: PASS — ALTER EXTENSION pgmnemo UPDATE TO '0.11.1' from 0.11.0 → version() = '0.11.1'

installcheck.flat_vs_delta_chain: pg_dump schema diff IDENTICAL (excluding pg_dump randomized session tokens) — extension/pgmnemo--0.11.1.sql produces the same schema as installing 0.11.0 then ALTER UPDATE TO 0.11.1; recall_fast carries p_content_types in both.

significance_note: Optional filter with a default-unchanged path; no recall-quality claim is made, so no z-test/p-value gate applies. Correctness (not quality) is the bar and is met by 36/36 installcheck including typed_recall_fast regression test and schema-identical flat-vs-delta verification.
